aliases: stir-fried water spinach with shrimp paste
food_subgroup: Stir-fried leafy greens with shrimp paste

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a water spinach concept but no specific stir-fried kangkung with belacan preparation. Nearest parent is a cooked vegetable dish. Recommend new concept.
ontology.foodex2_description: Vegetable dishes — stir-fried leafy greens with seasoning (closest parent)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Stir-fried Kangkung with Belacan as a side dish at a Singapore hawker centre weighs approximately 200g and consists of fresh water spinach stir-fried at high heat with toasted belacan, dried shrimp, garlic, and chilli, with a slight char and intensely savoury-spicy flavour.

nutrient_source.title: Stir-fried Kangkung with Belacan — HPB Singapore Foodo Database; no exact code found; values adapted from HPB stir-fried leafy vegetables and kangkung composition data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact stir-fried kangkung belacan entry found in HPB Foodo. Values adapted from HPB kangkung (water spinach) raw composition data plus stir-fry oil and belacan seasoning correction.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium approximately 1024mg per serving (200g) — about 45% of the 2300mg daily recommended limit, driven by belacan (shrimp paste) and dried shrimp.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request reduced belacan in the preparation; kangkung itself is very low in sodium and provides potassium which helps counteract dietary sodium.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Very low carbohydrate content (~3.5g/100g); negligible glycaemic impact; high fibre for a cooked vegetable side dish.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Stir-fried Kangkung with Belacan is an excellent vegetable side dish for diabetic patients; it adds minimal carbohydrates and calories while contributing high folate, vitamins A and C, and dietary fibre.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Kangkung is exceptionally rich in vitamin A (beta-carotene), folate, and vitamin C — all with anti-inflammatory and immune-supporting properties; cooking oil adds healthy unsaturated fats.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Stir-fried Kangkung with Belacan is one of the best vegetable dishes for patients with rheumatoid arthritis; the high beta-carotene and folate content support immune regulation, and the high potassium content supports cardiovascular health.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Stir-fried Kangkung with Belacan is one of Singapore's most popular vegetable side dishes — fresh water spinach (kangkung; Ipomoea aquatica) stir-fried in a blazing-hot wok with pounded toasted belacan (fermented shrimp paste), dried shrimp, garlic, and fresh or dried chilli until wilted with a slight char and an intensely savoury, umami-rich, spicy flavour. Kangkung is a semi-aquatic tropical leafy green with hollow stems and tender leaves, exceptional in vitamin A, folate, and vitamin C. It is a staple side dish at zi char (Chinese stir-fry) stalls and Malay hawker stalls throughout Singapore and Malaysia.
common_user_queries: how many calories in stir-fried kangkung
kangkung vs kailan which is healthier

confidence_reason: No exact stir-fried kangkung belacan entry found in HPB Foodo. Values adapted from HPB kangkung composition data with belacan and oil correction. Confidence medium.
notes: Vitamin A (beta-carotene) content is notably high — kangkung is one of the richest plant sources of provitamin A in the Singapore diet. Folate content is clinically significant. The belacan is a crustacean allergen. Sodium content varies significantly based on belacan quantity used.
